summary: Enhancement 11 (Settings — Account Management CRUD) tested end-to-end. Backend: 12/12 pytest tests PASSED in 7.65s covering /api/auth/change-password (no-token 401, wrong current 400, short new 422, letters-only new 422, same-password 400, change+login+restore success, audit_log failure case) and /api/auth/delete-account (lowercase confirm 422, wrong password 400, full delete with 2 seeded scoped records returning ok+collections_cleared+total_records, post-delete login 401) plus regression (login + /me). Audit logs verified directly in Mongo: 3 password_changed + 5 password_change_failed events keyed on test1's user_id, and 2 account_deleted events for the temp users. Frontend: logged in test1@plos.app/test123 → /settings, all required testIDs visible (profile-card, edit-profile-btn, open-change-password, open-delete-account, pw-*, del-*). Profile edit saved Atlanta/GA/DeKalb and the location chip in the card updated to 'Atlanta, GA, DeKalb County'. Change-password modal: mismatched confirm shows inline 'New password and confirmation do not match.'; valid submit (test123 → NewPlos2026) closed the modal successfully. Delete modal: 2-step flow works — lowercase 'delete' shows 'You must type DELETE exactly (capital letters) to continue.', 'DELETE' advances to step 2 (del-password / del-back / del-submit visible), del-back returns to step 1, re-advance shows 'Permanently Delete' button text, del-close exits without submission. test1 password restored to 'test123' at the end via direct Mongo update (UI rollback path is blocked by the Pydantic min_length=8 on new_password — see backend_issues). Final login with test1@plos.app/test123 confirmed 200. KNOWN BUG: /api/auth/change-password rejects any new_password < 8 chars with 422 before the same-password 400 check fires; combined with test1 having a 7-char password this breaks the 'restore via API' workflow the spec assumed.

minor.issue: Pydantic Field(min_length=8) on new_password fires before any business rule. Spec test case `current=test123,new=test123` expects 400 with 'New password must be different…' but actually returns 422 because the 7-char new_password fails min_length first. Same root cause means an account with a <8 char legacy password cannot be 'restored' to that password via the endpoint — locking out test1 unless we bypass via direct DB write. Either (a) drop min_length=8 in favor of a field_validator that runs after the same-password check, or (b) document that the same-password 400 is only reachable for >=8 char passwords (which is fine, but then the spec test #5 should use an 8+ char password).

Symmetric issue with rolling back from a new strong password back to the legacy 'test123'. Frontend at /app/frontend/app/settings.tsx:112 enforces the same 8-char min before calling the API, so even via UI the rollback path documented in the test spec cannot succeed. Recommend the spec/UX use an >=8 char baseline password going forward.

action_items: Decide policy on new_password length: either keep min_length=8 (and update spec/test cases that exercise the 'same password' branch to use an >=8 char baseline so the 400 branch is reachable), OR replace Field(min_length=8) with a field_validator that runs *after* the same-password check so the 400 'different from current' message wins when both are short/equal.

Consider exposing an internal admin/dev password-reset shortcut (or seed script) for legacy short passwords so testing agents don't have to write directly to Mongo to restore test1 after a change-password rollout — currently the test fixture is fragile.
critical_code_review_comments: account_mgmt.py is clean and tight: dynamic enumeration of collections for cascade-delete protects against forgetting to update a static map, audit_logs is preserved (skipped), users is deleted last. Bcrypt hashing is reused from server.py via dependency injection — no duplicate crypto code.

delete_account computes total_records via sum(deletion_report.values()) — correct; includes the 'users' parent row count (1) when applicable, so total >= 2 with even a single scoped seed record. Matches the spec contract.

Frontend settings.tsx wires both modals with proper inline-error UX and testIDs. The 2-step delete flow keeps step1 confirm text in state when navigating back, which is good UX. submitDelete correctly signs out + navigates to /(auth)/login on success.

audit_logs writes are wrapped in try/except so they never break the main flow — good. password_change_failed event is written on bad current password; account_delete_failed on bad password; account_deleted on success. password_changed audit event has empty details payload — consider adding a hashed-truncated request_ip or user_agent for forensic tracing (low priority).
No deprecated Expo packages or RN component issues spotted in settings.tsx. SafeAreaView is correctly imported from react-native-safe-area-context. All text is wrapped in <Text>.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — profile edit, change-password (mismatch + success), delete 2-step modal (lowercase err, advance, back nav, close) all verified; 0 console errors

seed_data_creation: Each delete-account test registers a fresh e11_del_<random>@plos.app temp user via /api/auth/register and seeds two scoped records (world-clock Tokyo + deal-finder search) before exercising the delete endpoint. All temp users were deleted by the test itself. test1@plos.app's password was rotated test123 → NewPlos2026 during testing and restored to test123 via direct Mongo write at the end (UI/API rollback blocked by min_length=8 — see backend_issues).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test1@plos.app password is 'test123' (verified final 200 login). If you need to change-password and roll back to 'test123', the endpoint will reject the rollback with 422 due to Field(min_length=8) on new_password — use a direct Mongo update (/tmp/restore_test1.py exists, or inline the bcrypt write) instead. /api/auth/delete-account requires confirm_text exactly 'DELETE' (case-sensitive). The delete handler dynamically enumerates collections via db.list_collection_names() and skips audit_logs — so audit trail survives even after account deletion.
rca_of_issue: Spec/implementation mismatch on change-password 'same password' check: Pydantic v2 Field constraints run during request body parsing, BEFORE any custom @field_validator AND before route handler code. Field(min_length=8) on new_password therefore short-circuits with 422 whenever the body has a <8 char new_password — including the spec's documented current=test123/new=test123 case. Mitigation in tests: split the spec assertion into two parts — (5a) reproduce the documented spec call and assert 422 (documenting the mismatch), and (5b) re-exercise the same-password branch with NewPlos2026/NewPlos2026 (both >=8 chars) so the 400 'different from current' branch is genuinely covered. Then restore test1's password via direct Mongo write (bypassing the 8-char gate) to keep downstream tests usable.
